question.label: First growth move
question.name: What is the first shift you want to make before we meet again?
question.description: Closes by turning the conversation into one concrete next move he owns.

answer: I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.

assessment.note: He named a specific next growth move and a concrete stakeholder milestone, indicating positive growth direction and clearer near-term focus.

history.answer_excerpt: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I should delegate more of the hands-on work to create room for that.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
A clear growth contract with milestones would keep me accountable.
I want to own one strategic bet end-to-end and set a milestone check-in with stakeholders in two weeks.
